content.content:
# CLAUDE.md — designflow-tracking

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first — it is the canonical guide. This file holds only
Claude Code-specific notes. For cross-repo infrastructure/runtime standards, see
[u2giants/albert-standards `infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/blob/main/infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md)
and update it when Cloud Run, Cloud Build, domains, BFF routing, secrets/env,
storage, auth/OIDC, or database operating rules change.

## Memory and branch

- User memory: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`. Work only on `sandbox-albert` (and `develop` for syncing).
  Before new code, confirm local `sandbox-albert`, fetch GitHub, merge `origin/develop` into
  local `sandbox-albert`, push that updated `sandbox-albert` to GitHub, then pull GitHub
  `sandbox-albert` locally before editing. Never commit to or merge into `main`. Commit locally,
  push to `origin/sandbox-albert`, then ship via PR `sandbox-albert` -> `develop`, updating or
  creating it after pushes.

## Ignore guidance

Use `.claudeignore` (mirrors `.cursorignore`). It excludes `node_modules/`, `dist/`, caches,
logs, and Playwright artifacts from AI context.

## Allowed operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, `npm install`, `npm test`, `node`, `git add/commit`,
  `git push origin sandbox-albert`.
- Not allowed: editing `node_modules/`; `git push --force`; committing to `main`.

## Tests

- `npm test` (Jest, `tests/**/*.test.js`). Run before every commit — `start:*` scripts run
  `npm test` first, so a red suite blocks boot.
- Add/extend a unit test for any function you add or change (mock Sequelize via
  `jest.mock('../../models/db')` and `helpers/auth`; see `tests/unit/sample.model.test.js`).

## Deployment / SSH

Deployment is Cloud Build → Artifact Registry → Cloud Run (see `AGENTS.md` §11). SSH is **not**
part of the workflow.

## Commit style

```
type(scope): short description
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `chore`. Scope e.g. `sample`, `tracking`, `migration`.

## Claude-specific behaviors

- Keep the `models/db.js` synchronous-populate-then-await ordering (see AGENTS.md §9).
- snake_case columns only in raw `ALTER TABLE` DDL.
- Derive sample status changes from `helpers/sampleStatus.js`; keep the frontend mirror
  (`pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts`) in sync.

content.text:
**Major finding that resolves my confusion:** the live revision `00023` was built from commit `951c718`, which **does** include my db.js fix (it's a descendant of `6d22d93` on `sandbox-albert`). So my connection fix *was* deployed — and `/sample/list` is still 30s. That definitively confirms: **the database query itself is the bottleneck**, not the connection.

The evidence is now unambiguous:
- Empty result → 13–40ms (fast)
- Has data → 10–31s (slow)

Cost that scales with row data = a **bad query plan**, classic for `findAndCountAll` with `distinct: true` over a `hasMany` JOIN (the `sample_attachment` photo join). That join forces Postgres to join every attachment, dedupe, and run a `COUNT(DISTINCT …)`.

Let me check whether a build for my diagnostic push is queued before deciding next steps.

cd /c/repos/dflow/designflow-tracking
git add models/sample.model.js models/db.js tests/unit/sample.model.test.js tests/unit/db.migration.test.js
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(sample): make /sample/list fast — drop distinct+JOIN, batch photo lookup

THE actual fix for "Failed to load samples". Cloud Run logs proved /sample/list
was fast when empty (~20ms) but 10-31s once samples had data, consistently —
the signature of a bad query plan, not a connection issue (the earlier db.js
pool change did not help; confirmed deployed on rev 00023 and still 30s).

Cause: Sample.list used findAndCountAll({ distinct:true, include: sample_attachment
(hasMany) }). That makes Postgres LEFT JOIN every attachment, de-duplicate, and run
COUNT(DISTINCT sample_id_pk) over the join — which scales badly and blew past the
BFF's 30s proxy timeout.

Fix:
- Page query is now a plain paginated scan of `sample` (no include, no distinct):
  COUNT(*) + ORDER BY sample_id_pk DESC LIMIT uses the PK index.
- primary_photo is resolved via a separate, batched, index-backed lookup on
  sample_attachment (same shape as the existing box-label lookup), preferring the
  primary_image flag. totalCount semantics unchanged (distinct sample count == COUNT(*)).
- Add idempotent FK indexes (sample_attachment.sample_id_fk, sample_event.sample_id_fk,
  sample.box_id_fk) so the batched lookups stay fast as data grows.

Tests: page query asserted to have no include/distinct; batched photo mapping
(prefers primary_image; null when none); index presence. Full suite 97/97.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git push origin sandbox-albert 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text:
I've found and fixed the real problem, but hit a separate infrastructure issue I need your decision on. Let me lay it out plainly.

## What was actually wrong (and why my first fix failed)
My first fix (the database "connection" change) was a wrong guess — I've confirmed it deployed and the page was *still* 30 seconds. The logs proved the real cause: the samples list was **fast when empty (~20ms) but 10–31 seconds whenever there was actual data**. That pattern means the **database query itself was badly written**, not a connection problem.

The samples list was asking the database to join in every sample's photos and de-duplicate the whole thing just to show the grid — which gets dramatically slower as soon as samples have photos, and tipped over the 30-second limit → "Failed to load samples."

## The real fix (done + tested)
I rewrote the query so it:
- loads the samples list as a simple, fast, indexed page (no expensive join), and
- fetches the thumbnail photos separately in one small batched lookup, plus added database indexes so it stays fast as data grows.

This is committed, pushed, and covered by tests (**97/97 passing**, including a guard so this exact slow pattern can't come back).

## The blocker I need you to decide on
Your sandbox normally **auto-publishes** code after each push. That auto-publisher (the Cloud Build trigger) **has stopped working** — it fired for every push earlier today, but now it's gone (I confirmed the trigger no longer exists). So my fix is saved on GitHub but **hasn't gone live on your sandbox yet**, which is why you'd still see the old behavior.

I don't want to force a deploy or push toward production without your say-so. How do you want to get the fix live?

content.text:
The fix is now **live on your sandbox** — revision `popcre-albert-tracking-sandbox-00024-p7k`, running the corrected code (and the registry/trigger detour is sorted out). Working tree is clean.

I was wrong once, so I won't declare victory until we see it. Two things:

**Please reload the Sample Tracking page** on your sandbox (`alsand.designflow.app`) and tell me if it loads. Then I'll confirm the actual query time from the server logs to be sure it's genuinely fixed (not just "looks better").

While you do that, here's the honest recap of what happened:

- **My first fix was a wrong guess** (a database *connection* tweak). I confirmed it deployed and the page was still ~30s, which disproved it.
- **The real cause:** the samples list query was forcing the database to join in every photo and de-duplicate the whole set just to draw the grid — fast when empty, but 10–30s once samples had data, which tripped the 30-second timeout → "Failed to load samples."
- **The real fix (now live):** load the list as a simple fast page, fetch thumbnails in a separate small batched lookup, and add database indexes. Fully tested (97/97).
- **Bonus problem I hit:** your sandbox's auto-deploy was broken today (the deploy trigger was deleted and the image registry moved regions), which is why nothing was going live. I deployed manually instead. You'll likely want someone to restore that auto-deploy trigger — but that's infrastructure, separate from the app code.

Once you confirm the page loads, I'll verify the timing in the logs and correct the documentation (which currently still describes my first, wrong diagnosis).

content.text: Found a real problem: the container's start command is **`npm test && nodemon index.js`** — every cold start runs the *entire test suite* and then serves via `nodemon` (a dev file-watcher). That's a deployment anti-pattern and a big chunk of the 9.66s. Let me confirm from the container's startup logs.

content.text:
**That's the smoking gun.** The startup logs show the request that took 9.66s was *waiting for the container to boot* — and boot ran the **entire Jest suite** (finished 21:22:09) then started **nodemon** (21:22:10), then the server became ready (21:22:12) and answered instantly. So:

- The **query fix works** — once the server was up, it answered immediately (the slow query is gone).
- The remaining ~9s is **cold-start**: Cloud Run scales to zero, and every cold start runs the full test suite + nodemon before serving. Worse, if a test ever failed, the container would **never serve** → total outage.

So the original "Failed to load samples" (30s timeout) is fixed, but first-load is still ~9s and fragile. Let me fix the boot: run tests at **build** time (still gates deploy) and run the server with plain `node` at runtime. Let me read the Dockerfile.

file.content:
FROM node:lts-alpine as builder
ARG NODE_ENV
ENV NODE_ENV $NODE_ENV
RUN apk add --update imagemagick
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN mkdir -p .yarn-cache && yarn --cache-folder .yarn-cache --production=false
EXPOSE 5000
CMD yarn start:$NODE_ENV

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md — designflow-tracking

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first — it is the canonical guide. This file holds only
Claude Code-specific notes. For cross-repo infrastructure/runtime standards, see
[u2giants/albert-standards `infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/blob/main/infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md)
and update it when Cloud Run, Cloud Build, domains, BFF routing, secrets/env,
storage, auth/OIDC, or database operating rules change.

## Memory and branch

- User memory: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`. Work only on `sandbox-albert` (and `develop` for syncing).
  Before new code, confirm local `sandbox-albert`, fetch GitHub, merge `origin/develop` into
  local `sandbox-albert`, push that updated `sandbox-albert` to GitHub, then pull GitHub
  `sandbox-albert` locally before editing. Never commit to or merge into `main`. Commit locally,
  push to `origin/sandbox-albert`, then ship via PR `sandbox-albert` -> `develop`, updating or
  creating it after pushes.

## Ignore guidance

Use `.claudeignore` (mirrors `.cursorignore`). It excludes `node_modules/`, `dist/`, caches,
logs, and Playwright artifacts from AI context.

## Allowed operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, `npm install`, `npm test`, `node`, `git add/commit`,
  `git push origin sandbox-albert`.
- Not allowed: editing `node_modules/`; `git push --force`; committing to `main`.

## Tests

- `npm test` (Jest, `tests/**/*.test.js`). Run before every commit — `start:*` scripts run
  `npm test` first, so a red suite blocks boot.
- Add/extend a unit test for any function you add or change (mock Sequelize via
  `jest.mock('../../models/db')` and `helpers/auth`; see `tests/unit/sample.model.test.js`).

## Deployment / SSH

Deployment is Cloud Build → Artifact Registry → Cloud Run (see `AGENTS.md` §11). SSH is **not**
part of the workflow.

## Commit style

```
type(scope): short description
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `chore`. Scope e.g. `sample`, `tracking`, `migration`.

## Claude-specific behaviors

- Keep the `models/db.js` synchronous-populate-then-await ordering (see AGENTS.md §9).
- snake_case columns only in raw `ALTER TABLE` DDL.
- Derive sample status changes from `helpers/sampleStatus.js`; keep the frontend mirror
  (`pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts`) in sync.

RUN mkdir -p .yarn-cache && yarn --cache-folder .yarn-cache --production=false
# Run the unit suite at BUILD time so a red suite fails the image build (and thus
# blocks the deploy). Previously tests ran inside the runtime start script, which
# meant every Cloud Run cold start re-ran the whole suite before serving traffic
# (~adds seconds to first-load) and a flaky test would stop the container from
# ever booting. Gate stays; the cold-start penalty and outage risk go away.
RUN yarn test
EXPOSE 5000
CMD yarn start:$NODE_ENV

content.text: Tests pass and the deployed start scripts now use plain `node`. Let me commit and push, then rebuild + redeploy to the sandbox.

input.command:
cd /c/repos/dflow/designflow-tracking
git add Dockerfile package.json
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
perf(boot): run tests at build time, serve with node (not test+nodemon per cold start)

The deployed container CMD was `yarn start:<env>` = `npm test && nodemon index.js`,
so every Cloud Run cold start re-ran the full Jest suite and then served via the
nodemon dev watcher before answering the first request. Combined with scale-to-zero
this added ~9s to first-load (measured: a /sample/list request waited the whole boot)
and meant any flaky test would stop the container from ever booting (outage).

- Dockerfile: `RUN yarn test` at build time — a red suite still fails the image build
  and blocks the deploy (gate preserved), but runs once per build, not per cold start.
- package.json: start:production/sandbox/sandbox-albert now `node index.js` (no test,
  no nodemon). Localhost `start` keeps nodemon for dev.

Pairs with the /sample/list query fix; together first-load drops from ~30s (timeout
failure) to a fast warm response and a much smaller cold start.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git rev-parse --short HEAD
git push origin sandbox-albert 2>&1 | tail -2

Both fixes are now live on the sandbox — revision `popcre-albert-tracking-sandbox-00025-vgn`. The boot logs confirm the container now starts **straight to the server** (no test suite, no nodemon on startup — it came up on the first probe).

So two real fixes are deployed and verified at the infrastructure level:
1. **The slow query** — confirmed gone (the earlier 9.66s request answered the *instant* the server finished booting, so the query itself is now fast).
2. **The slow/fragile cold start** — the container no longer runs the whole test suite before serving.

**Could you reload the Sample Tracking page once more and tell me if it loads quickly?** Then I'll confirm the exact timing from the logs and finalize.

Two notes on what you flagged:
- **The frontend being stuck on `7c477de` (3 days old) is real but separate** — it's the *frontend* app, and my fix is in the *backend*. The old frontend still calls the same `/sample/list`, which is now fast, so samples should load regardless. 
- **Why everything's stale:** your sandbox's automatic deployments are broken right now — the deploy trigger was deleted and the image registry was moved (us‑central1 → us‑east4) earlier today. That's why nothing (frontend or backend) has auto-updated. I deployed the backend manually. **Someone should restore the sandbox auto-deploy + re-point the frontend** — that's infrastructure (your Ansible/ops process), outside the app code, so I haven't touched it.

Once you confirm it loads, I'll correct the documentation (it currently still reflects my first, wrong diagnosis) and give you a final summary.

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md — Claude Code notes for designflow-frontend

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first. It is the canonical guide for repo structure, custom-code boundaries, current architecture, incidents, deployment, and pending work. This file is only for Claude Code-specific operating notes. For cross-repo infrastructure/runtime standards, see [u2giants/albert-standards `infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/blob/main/infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md) and update it when Cloud Run, Cloud Build, domains, BFF routing, secrets/env, storage, auth/OIDC, or database operating rules change.

## Memory and Branch

- User memory file: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Commit and push only to `sandbox-albert`
- Before new code, confirm local `sandbox-albert`, fetch GitHub, merge `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, push that updated `sandbox-albert` to GitHub, then pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally before editing.
- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; use `develop` only for sync/PR base. Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`.
- Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, push to `origin/sandbox-albert`, then keep the GitHub PR `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated, or create it if missing.

## Ignore Guidance

Use `.claudeignore`. It matches `.cursorignore` and excludes generated, vendored, cache, asset, and legacy/demo template paths such as `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `.angular/`, `.cache/`, `.yarn/`, `coverage/`, `src/assets/`, `src/static-data/`, and Vex demo pages.

## Allowed Operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, run `yarn`/`npm` scripts, run `ng`, use `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push origin sandbox-albert`
- Not allowed by default: force-push, rewrite history, edit `node_modules/` or `dist/`
- SSH is not the normal deployment path. Deployment is Cloud Build -> Docker -> Cloud Run after branch push; use `gcloud` only for explicit rollback or incident triage.

## Tests

```powershell
yarn test
yarn test --testPathPatterns="rfq.grid.config"
yarn test:watch
```

Run the relevant tests before committing. Add or update unit tests for every new or changed function/behavior. The Docker build also runs `yarn test`.

## Claude-Specific Rules

- Use the AG Grid MCP docs before touching AG Grid code: set version `35.1.0`, framework `angular`; the repo uses AG Grid `35.2.1`.
- AG Grid modules are registered in ONE place: `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts` (read its header). It currently registers `AllEnterpriseModule`. If that ever gets trimmed to a hand-picked list, any new Enterprise feature you use MUST be added there or it silently no-ops in prod. Never register AG Grid modules anywhere else.
- Do not use `.toPromise()` in new code; use `firstValueFrom()`.
- Do not add new `window['anything']` globals; use Angular DI.
- Do not add `aggFunc` to RFQ grid columns without reading `AGENTS.md`.
- For `mat-select`, keep options in a synchronous array, do not mix `[formControl]` with `[(value)]`, and never add `(click)="select.open()"`.

## Commit Style

```text
type(scope): short description
```

Use `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, or `chore`. Keep messages specific.

content.text:
All four changes are done, tested (10 new tests, full sample-tracking suite 25/25 green), committed, and pushed — PR [#124](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/124) is updated.

## What I changed in the New Sample dialog

1. **Add a photo** — there's now an optional "Add photo" button with a thumbnail preview. Since a sample has no ID until it's created, the dialog just holds the file; right after the sample is created, the photo is uploaded and set as its primary image (reusing the same upload flow the detail screen uses).

2. **Squished / scrollbars** — widened the dialog to 760px and laid the fields out in a **two-column grid** so it fits without an inner scrollbar (collapses to one column on small screens).

3. **The "Office" dropdown** — to answer your question: that field is `office_location`, meaning **which office physically holds the sample right now — Ningbo or NYC**. I relabeled it to **"Current office (where the sample is now)"** and added a hint line, so it's no longer cryptic.

4. **"Next stop" is now optional** — it's no longer required. If you leave it blank, it now means "ship straight to the final destination," and on save the next stop is automatically set to the final destination. **Final destination** is now the one required route field.

## One thing before you can see it
These are **frontend** changes, and your sandbox frontend is the one stuck 3 days old (`7c477de`) because the auto-deploy is broken — same root issue as the backend. So they won't appear on `alsand` until the frontend is deployed.

I can deploy the frontend manually now, the same way I did the backend (it's a heavier Angular build, ~several minutes). Want me to go ahead?

steps:
  # Step 1: Build Docker Image
  - id: Build
    name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    args:
      - build
      - -t
      - ${_IMAGE}
      - --build-arg=NODE_ENV=${_APP_ENV}
      - --build-arg=COMMIT_SHA=${COMMIT_SHA}

    name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    args:
      - push
      - ${_IMAGE}
    waitFor:

    args:

      - --region=${_REGION}
      - --port=${_PORT}

The frontend Docker build runs the full test suite as a gate, and a **pre-existing, unrelated test is broken**: `src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.spec.ts` → `comp.loadRFQSteps is not a function` (2 failures out of 664; my sample-tracking tests pass). This stale RFQ test is blocking *all* frontend deploys. Let me look at it to see how serious it is.

content.text: `loadRFQSteps()` was intentionally **refactored away** (merged into `loadInitialMetadata()` + `applyRFQSteps()` in a recent perf commit), but the spec wasn't updated — so it calls a method that no longer exists. This stale test is almost certainly why your frontend auto-deploy has been red/stale for days. It's a clean maintenance fix. Let me read the test and the new code to fix it correctly.

The fix is clean: the cache-population logic now lives in `applyRFQSteps(data)` (takes the data directly instead of fetching). I'll update the stale test to call the new method, preserving the same two assertions.

file.content:
# ==========================
# Stage 1: Build Angular App
# ==========================
FROM node:lts AS builder

# Set build-time environment variables
ARG NODE_ENV
ENV NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV}
ARG COMMIT_SHA=local

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Activate Yarn 4 (Berry) — lockfile is in v4 format; node:lts ships corepack
RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare yarn@4.13.0 --activate

# Install dependencies (leverage caching for faster builds)
COPY package.json yarn.lock .yarnrc.yml ./
RUN yarn install --immutable

# Copy source code, run tests, then build Angular app
COPY . .
RUN TS=$(node -e " \
      const d=new Date(); \
      const date=d.toLocaleDateString('en-CA',{timeZone:'America/New_York'}); \
      const time=d.toLocaleTimeString('en-US',{timeZone:'America/New_York',hour:'numeric',minute:'2-digit',hour12:true,timeZoneName:'short'}); \
      console.log(date+' '+time);") && \
    printf 'export const APP_VERSION = {\n  commitHash: '"'"'%s'"'"',\n  pushedAt: '"'"'%s'"'"',\n};\n' \
    "$(echo "${COMMIT_SHA}" | cut -c1-7)" "$TS" > src/app/version.ts
RUN yarn test
RUN yarn build:${NODE_ENV}


# ===============================
# Stage 2: Serve App with NGINX
# ===============================
FROM macbre/nginx-brotli:1.19.10

# Copy custom NGINX configuration
COPY --from=builder /app/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

# Copy built Angular app to NGINX html directory
COPY --from=builder /app/dist/browser /usr/share/nginx/html

# Expose HTTP port
EXPOSE 80

# Default command provided by the base NGINX image

content.text: The Dockerfile runs `yarn test` then `yarn build`. My local type-check error is a pnpm-vs-yarn install discrepancy (local uses `.pnpm`, Docker uses yarn) — it didn't block the Docker build, which ran all 664 tests and failed only on the 2 `loadRFQSteps` assertions I just fixed. My edit only renames a test call, so it'll compile in Docker. Let me commit and re-run the real build (which validates both `yarn test` and `yarn build`).

input.command:
cd /c/repos/dflow/designflow-frontend
git add src/app/pages/rfq/rfq.component.spec.ts
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
test(rfq): update stale loadRFQSteps spec to applyRFQSteps after refactor

loadRFQSteps() was merged into loadInitialMetadata()'s forkJoin (2026-06-22) and
its cache-population logic moved to applyRFQSteps(data). The spec still called the
removed loadRFQSteps(), failing with "is not a function" and blocking the frontend
build's `yarn test` gate (and thus every deploy). Point the two cases at
applyRFQSteps(data) — same assertions, now passing data directly instead of fetching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git rev-parse --short HEAD
git push origin sandbox-albert 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text:
Done — **the frontend is live on your sandbox.** Revision `popcre-albert-frontend-sandbox-00225-pkd` is serving (HTTP 200), built from your commit `4625e38c` (the New Sample changes).

👉 **Hard-refresh the page** (Ctrl+Shift+R) when you open it — it's a single-page app, so your browser may be holding the old bundle.

## What you'll see on the New Sample screen
1. **Add photo** — optional "Add photo" button with a thumbnail preview; it uploads as the sample's primary image right after creation.
2. **No more squish/scroll** — wider dialog (760px) with fields in two columns.
3. **"Office" is now clear** — relabeled **"Current office (where the sample is now)"** with a hint. (It means which office physically holds the sample — Ningbo or NYC.)
4. **"Next stop" is optional** — blank = ship straight to the final destination (it auto-fills to the final destination on save). Final destination is the one required route field.

## What I had to fix along the way
- The first frontend build **failed on a pre-existing broken test** — `rfq.component.spec.ts` was calling `loadRFQSteps()`, a method that had been refactored away days ago. **That stale test was blocking every frontend deploy** (very likely why your sandbox was stuck 3 days). I updated it to match the current code and it's green now.
- Since your sandbox **auto-deploy is still broken** (deleted trigger + registry moved to us‑east4), I deployed manually — both backend (earlier) and now frontend.

## Still needs your team's attention (infrastructure, not app code)
Your sandbox's automatic deploys are down. Someone should **restore the Cloud Build trigger and point it at the new us‑east4 registry**, so future pushes deploy on their own instead of needing manual builds. That's an ops/Ansible task, outside the app repos, so I left it for you.

All changes are committed and pushed on `sandbox-albert` (PRs [#124](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/124) frontend, [#17](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-tracking/pull/17) backend). Give it a hard refresh and let me know how the New Sample screen looks.

43	    agent, Realtime watcher, and Supabase Edge Functions. No Sequelize/`pg` dependency or
44	    persistent direct-Postgres pool was found in the scanned PopDAM runtime packages.
45	  - `u2giants/directus` has one direct `pg` caller in `pm-system/sync-plm-masters.mjs`, but it

48	    long-lived shared-Supabase pool and is not exposed to this specific idle pooler failure mode.

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md — Claude Code notes for designflow-frontend

Read [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) first. It is the canonical guide for repo structure, custom-code boundaries, current architecture, incidents, deployment, and pending work. This file is only for Claude Code-specific operating notes. For cross-repo infrastructure/runtime standards, see [u2giants/albert-standards `infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md`](https://github.com/u2giants/albert-standards/blob/main/infrastructure/designflow-cloud-run.md) and update it when Cloud Run, Cloud Build, domains, BFF routing, secrets/env, storage, auth/OIDC, or database operating rules change.

## Memory and Branch

- User memory file: `C:\Users\ahazan2\.claude\projects\D--repos-dflow\memory\MEMORY.md`
- Active branch: `sandbox-albert`
- Commit and push only to `sandbox-albert`
- Before new code, confirm local `sandbox-albert`, fetch GitHub, merge `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, push that updated `sandbox-albert` to GitHub, then pull GitHub `sandbox-albert` locally before editing.
- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; use `develop` only for sync/PR base. Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`.
- Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, push to `origin/sandbox-albert`, then keep the GitHub PR `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated, or create it if missing.

## Ignore Guidance

Use `.claudeignore`. It matches `.cursorignore` and excludes generated, vendored, cache, asset, and legacy/demo template paths such as `dist/`, `node_modules/`, `.angular/`, `.cache/`, `.yarn/`, `coverage/`, `src/assets/`, `src/static-data/`, and Vex demo pages.

## Allowed Operations

- Allowed: read/edit repo files, run `yarn`/`npm` scripts, run `ng`, use `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push origin sandbox-albert`
- Not allowed by default: force-push, rewrite history, edit `node_modules/` or `dist/`
- SSH is not the normal deployment path. Deployment is Cloud Build -> Docker -> Cloud Run after branch push; use `gcloud` only for explicit rollback or incident triage.

## Tests

```powershell
yarn test
yarn test --testPathPatterns="rfq.grid.config"
yarn test:watch
```

Run the relevant tests before committing. Add or update unit tests for every new or changed function/behavior. The Docker build also runs `yarn test`.

## Claude-Specific Rules

- Use the AG Grid MCP docs before touching AG Grid code: set version `35.1.0`, framework `angular`; the repo uses AG Grid `35.2.1`.
- AG Grid modules are registered in ONE place: `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/ag-grid-modules.ts` (read its header). It currently registers `AllEnterpriseModule`. If that ever gets trimmed to a hand-picked list, any new Enterprise feature you use MUST be added there or it silently no-ops in prod. Never register AG Grid modules anywhere else.
- Do not use `.toPromise()` in new code; use `firstValueFrom()`.
- Do not add new `window['anything']` globals; use Angular DI.
- Do not add `aggFunc` to RFQ grid columns without reading `AGENTS.md`.
- For `mat-select`, keep options in a synchronous array, do not mix `[formControl]` with `[(value)]`, and never add `(click)="select.open()"`.

## Commit Style

```text
type(scope): short description
```

Use `fix`, `feat`, `refactor`, `docs`, or `chore`. Keep messages specific.

fix(sample): tighten New Sample dialog — compact density, no clipped label/scroll

Follow-up to the New Sample dialog: the fields were full-height (56px) with the
reserved hint/error space under each, so the form was oversized, had big gaps,
overflowed into a scrollbar, and the first outlined label was clipped at the top.

- Apply Material compact density (-4 → 40px fields) scoped to the dialog via a
  `compact-sample-dialog` panelClass (global styles.scss; Material renders field
  DOM outside the component's view encapsulation).
- subscriptSizing="dynamic" on every field removes the ~22px reserved subscript
  space, collapsing the wasted gaps between rows.
- Tighten the grid row gap and add top padding so the first field's floating
  label is no longer clipped by the dialog content edge.

Together these let the whole form fit without an inner scrollbar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git push origin sandbox-albert 2>&1 | tail -2

The tightening changes are committed and pushed (`3bd0094d`), and the frontend rebuild is running in the background (~7 min). Here's what I changed to address each point in your screenshot:

- **Clipped "Origin" label** → added top padding so the floating label clears the dialog's top edge.
- **Huge fields (3× too big)** → applied Material **compact density** (field height 56px → **40px**), scoped only to this dialog.
- **Wasted space between fields** → `subscriptSizing="dynamic"` removes the ~22px reserved hint area under every field, and I tightened the row gaps.
- **Scrollbar** → the height saved by the above (≈30px per field row × ~8 rows) should let the whole form fit without scrolling.

I'll confirm the deploy went live and report back as soon as the build finishes.

content.text:
Deployed and live — revision `popcre-albert-frontend-sandbox-00226-6n9`, image `manual-3bd0094d`, site serving HTTP 200.

👉 **Hard-refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R)** to pick up the new bundle, then reopen **New Sample**.

## What you should now see
- **"Origin" label no longer clipped** — added top breathing room so the floating label clears the dialog edge.
- **Fields ~30% shorter** — compact Material density (56px → 40px), scoped to this dialog only, so nothing else in the app changes.
- **Gaps closed up** — removed the empty reserved hint space under every field (`subscriptSizing="dynamic"`) and tightened the row spacing.
- **No scrollbar** — the height saved across ~8 rows should let the whole form fit.

Changes are committed/pushed on `sandbox-albert` (PR [#124](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/124)) and tests still pass (10/10 on the dialog spec).

If it's *still* not tight enough for your taste, I can push the density one more notch (-4 → -5, the Material minimum, ~36px fields) or drop the two-column rows to three-across for the short numeric fields — just say the word after you look.

content.text:
Now I have hard data. The real problems:
- **Label clip**: `mat-dialog-content` has `padding-top: 0px`, and the first field's floating label sits **7px above** the content's top edge → clipped. My earlier `padding-top` got overridden (computed 0).
- **Oversized fields**: every "half" field is **348px wide** regardless of content — Retail price (4 digits) gets 348px. The rigid 2-column grid forces equal halves.

Let me prototype the right layout — flex-wrap with content-appropriate widths — live in the browser to verify before committing:

input.command:
cd /c/repos/dflow/designflow-frontend
git add src/app/pages/sample_tracking/create-dialog/create-dialog.component.html \
        src/app/pages/sample_tracking/create-dialog/create-dialog.component.scss \
        src/styles.scss
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(sample): right-size New Sample fields (flex layout) + fix clipped label

Researched the live rendered dialog (measured field widths, label positions,
dialog padding) instead of guessing:
- The rigid 2-col grid forced every "half" field to 348px, so a 4-digit Retail
  price got a 348px box and short dropdowns were far wider than their values.
  Replaced with a flex-wrap layout where each field is sized to its label/content
  (numbers ~96-120px, courier 140px, route selects ~168px, text fields grow).
- The clipped "Origin" label: mat-dialog-content computed padding-top was 0, so
  the first outlined field's floating label (~7px above the field) was cut off.
  Added padding-top to the dialog content via the panelClass in global styles
  (the content element is outside the component's view encapsulation).
- Shortened the long "Current office (...)" and "Next stop (optional)" labels to
  "Current office" / "Next stop"; moved the explanations to hover tooltips.

Result (verified in-browser): fields sized to content, no inner scrollbar, label
no longer clipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git rev-parse --short HEAD
git push origin sandbox-albert 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text:
The redesign is committed, pushed (`dcb3cb99`), tests green (10/10), and the build is running (~7 min). This round I inspected the **live** dialog in your browser and measured the actual rendering, so the changes are grounded in facts, not guesses:

- **Why fields were huge:** the 2-column grid forced *every* half-field to **348px** — confirmed by measurement — so a 4-digit Retail price got a 348px box. Replaced with a flex layout where each field is sized to its label/content (numbers ~96–120px, route dropdowns ~168px, text fields grow).
- **Why the label clipped:** `mat-dialog-content` had `padding-top: 0`, and the "Origin" floating label sits **7px above** the field → cut off. Added top padding via the dialog's panel class.
- Shortened the over-long "Current office (…)" / "Next stop (optional)" labels and moved the explanations to hover tooltips.

I prototyped this layout live and screenshotted it before committing — it fit with no scrollbar and content-sized fields. Once the deploy lands I'll reload your browser, take a fresh screenshot to confirm the real deployed result, and report back.

content.text: Now I see it: my override of the dialog-content padding lost to Material's `!important`/token (computed still `0px`), and the label is 7px above the clip edge. The reliable fix is to pad the **inner `.form` div** (which I own — no Material competition) instead of the dialog content. And "Final destination" just needs a shorter label — you called it "Destination" yourself, so I'll use that. Let me apply both:

fix(sample): unclip Origin label + shorten Destination label to fit

  which fits with room to spare; full meaning kept in the hover tooltip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

content.text:
Done — and this time verified on the **live deployment**, not guessed. Revision `00228-7nn` is serving `eda80ef5`. Measured in your browser: **no scrollbar**, **"Origin" label clears the top** (3px gap), and the renamed **"Destination"** label fits.

(The dialog looks faded in the shot only because the capture caught a transition frame — the DOM measurements confirm it's rendered correctly.)

## What I fixed — grounded in measurement, not guesses
I opened the real dialog in your browser, measured the DOM, and prototyped each change live before committing:

| Your complaint | What I measured | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Fields 2–3× too big (Retail price for 4 digits) | The 2-col grid forced **every** half-field to **348px** | Flex layout, each field sized to its label/content — numbers ~96–120px, dropdowns ~140–200px, text fields grow |
| Dropdowns far wider than their values | True width is set by the **label** (e.g. "Final destination" = 108px), not the value | Sized each select to its label; shortened the longest labels |
| "Origin" label clipped | `mat-dialog-content` padding-top is **0** (Material token, couldn't override) and the floating label sits **7px above** the edge | Padded the inner form container I control → label clears |
| "Final destination" cut to "Final destinat…" | 108px label in a ~112px slot | Renamed to **"Destination"** (your word); full meaning in the hover tooltip |
| Scrollbar | — | Eliminated — form now fits (`scrollHeight == clientHeight`) |

Plus: compact density (40px fields), `subscriptSizing="dynamic"` to remove the reserved gaps, and hover tooltips on "Current office" / "Next stop" / "Destination" explaining each.

Committed and pushed on `sandbox-albert` (PR [#124](https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/124)), tests green (10/10), and deployed live. Hard-refresh if you still see the old version. Take a look and tell me if any field should be wider/narrower — easy to nudge now that the layout is flex-based.

31	## Affected apps
32	- Confirmed: **designflow-tracking** (PM/PIM tracking service). Implementation:
33	  `models/db.js`; guarded by `tests/unit/db.migration.test.js`; app commit `6d22d93` on
34	  `sandbox-albert`.
35	- Audited 2026-06-23:
36	  - `u2giants/popcrm-web` is a static browser app. It uses `@supabase/supabase-js` in
37	    `src/lib/supabase.ts` and has no Sequelize/`pg` dependency or server-side Postgres pool in
38	    the app repo.
39	  - `u2giants/poppim-web` is a static browser app. It uses `@supabase/supabase-js` in
40	    `src/lib/supabase.ts` and has no Sequelize/`pg` dependency or server-side Postgres pool in
41	    the app repo.
42	  - `u2giants/popdam3` uses `@supabase/supabase-js` for the browser, Railway worker, bridge
43	    agent, Realtime watcher, and Supabase Edge Functions. No Sequelize/`pg` dependency or
44	    persistent direct-Postgres pool was found in the scanned PopDAM runtime packages.
45	  - `u2giants/directus` has one direct `pg` caller in `pm-system/sync-plm-masters.mjs`, but it
46	    creates a single `pg.Client`, connects to the local Directus Postgres URL from
47	    `pm-system/run-plm-sync.sh`, runs one transaction, and calls `client.end()`. It is not a
48	    long-lived shared-Supabase pool and is not exposed to this specific idle pooler failure mode.
49	- Still applicable to any future backend that uses a persistent Sequelize/`pg` pool against the
50	  shared Supabase pooler. Audit new workers/services against the settings above before deploying.
51

66	that service's DB pool config against the settings above.
